name: USS Pueblo Seizure (Jan 23, 1968)
detail: DPRK naval vessels seized the US Navy intelligence ship USS Pueblo in international waters off Wonsan, capturing 83 crew members. DPRK held the crew for 11 months, forcing a US humiliation statement before release. The Pueblo remains in Pyongyang as a museum propaganda trophy — the only US Navy ship held captive by a foreign power.

name: Axe Murder Incident — Panmunjom (Aug 18, 1976)
detail: North Korean soldiers axed to death two US Army officers (Capt. Arthur Bonifas and Lt. Mark Barrett) in the Joint Security Area at Panmunjom during a routine tree-trimming operation. The US responded with Operation Paul Bunyan — a massive show of force including B-52 bombers. Kim Il-sung expressed 'regret' — an extremely rare conciliatory statement.

name: Rangoon Bombing — Assassination Attempt (Oct 9, 1983)
detail: DPRK agents bombed the National Martyrs' Mausoleum in Rangoon (Yangon), Myanmar, targeting visiting South Korean President Chun Doo-hwan. Chun survived; 21 people were killed including 4 South Korean cabinet ministers. Three DPRK agents were captured; two were executed in Myanmar. Burma severed diplomatic relations with DPRK. Considered one of the most audacious state-sponsored assassination attempts of the Cold War.

name: KAL Flight 858 Bombing (Nov 29, 1987)
detail: DPRK agents bombed Korean Air Flight 858, killing all 115 people on board. The bomb was planted by two DPRK agents posing as Japanese tourists; one agent (Kim Hyun-hee) was captured while the other committed suicide. Kim Hyun-hee confessed to being trained by DPRK intelligence. The bombing was designed to destabilize South Korea ahead of the 1988 Seoul Olympics.

name: ROKS Cheonan Sunk by DPRK Torpedo (Mar 26, 2010)
detail: ROK Navy corvette ROKS Cheonan sank in the Yellow Sea near Baengnyeong Island; 46 sailors killed. Multinational Joint Investigation Group concluded a North Korean CHT-02D torpedo detonated under the ship. DPRK denied involvement. South Korea suspended inter-Korean trade (5.24 measures) and closed the Kaesong complex temporarily. UNSC issued a statement of condemnation without naming DPRK.

name: Yeonpyeong Island Artillery Attack (Nov 23, 2010)
detail: KPA artillery fired approximately 170 shells onto South Korean-controlled Yeonpyeong Island, killing 2 marines and 2 civilians and injuring 19. First direct attack on a South Korean civilian area since the 1953 armistice. Occurred after ROK live-fire exercises near disputed maritime boundary. South Korea returned fire. President Obama deployed USS George Washington carrier strike group to the Yellow Sea.

name: Sony Pictures Cyber Attack (Nov 2014)
detail: DPRK's Lazarus Group hacked Sony Pictures Entertainment in retaliation for The Interview — a satirical film depicting Kim Jong-un's assassination. Hackers destroyed data on 70% of Sony's servers, released unreleased films and confidential employee data. Obama administration attributed the attack to DPRK and imposed additional sanctions. FBI confirmed DPRK attribution. Demonstrated offensive cyber capabilities against US companies.

name: Kim Jong-nam Assassination with VX Nerve Agent (Feb 13, 2017)
detail: Kim Jong-nam, Kim Jong-un's estranged half-brother, was killed at Kuala Lumpur International Airport using VX nerve agent — a chemical weapon of mass destruction. Two women (Vietnamese and Indonesian) were recruited unwittingly to apply the agent. Four DPRK suspects fled to Pyongyang. Malaysia expelled the DPRK ambassador. US Treasury sanctioned DPRK entities for using a chemical weapon in a third country.

name: Trash / Fecal Balloon Campaign (May–Jun 2024)
detail: DPRK launched thousands of balloons carrying trash, manure, cigarette butts, and scraps of paper into South Korean territory, citing South Korean activist groups launching anti-DPRK leaflet balloons across the border. South Korea temporarily suspended the inter-Korean military agreement and resumed loudspeaker broadcasts along the DMZ in response. The tit-for-tat balloon campaign intensified through summer 2024.

name: GPS Jamming Campaign against South Korea (Jan & May 2024)
detail: DPRK conducted sustained GPS jamming attacks disrupting navigation systems for civilian aircraft, ships, and vehicles in the Seoul metropolitan area and western sea region. In January 2024, over 130 civilian aircraft reported GPS interference. Similar attacks in May 2024 affected hundreds of vessels. South Korea's military filed formal complaints and condemned the interference as a violation of international telecommunications law.
